guide.title: Quality Management System Guide
guide.subtitle: Learn how to create, approve and distribute quality documents step by step.

creation.title: Creating a Document
creation.step1Desc: Enter a unique document code (e.g., DOC-001), document title and optionally a description to explain the document's purpose.
creation.step2Desc: Select the document type (Procedure, Instruction, or Form) and the department that owns the document.
creation.step3Desc: If you are uploading on behalf of another department, select the preparer department. Otherwise, leave it empty.
creation.step4Desc: Choose who will prepare and initially review the document. Defaults to the person uploading, but can be changed.
creation.step5Desc: Choose who will give the final approval. Only users with Manager or Admin roles are listed.
creation.step6Desc: Choose which departments and/or individual users should receive the document after it is published.
creation.step7Desc: Set the starting revision number (default is 0). After the first revision, this number increments automatically.
creation.step8Desc: Upload the document file by dragging and dropping or clicking to browse.
creation.step9Desc: Save as Draft to continue editing later, or Submit to send the document directly into the approval workflow.

approval.title: Approval Workflow
approval.subtitle: Documents go through a two-step approval process before publishing.
approval.samePersonNote: When the preparer and approver are the same person, only a single approval step is required.
approval.rejectionNote: Rejected documents are locked. A new revision must be created to continue.

lifecycle.title: Document Lifecycle
lifecycle.subtitle: Every document revision goes through these stages:
lifecycle.draft: The revision is created as a draft. It can be edited and saved multiple times before submitting.
lifecycle.pendingApproval: The document has been submitted for approval. The assigned preparer receives a notification to review.
lifecycle.preparerApproved: The preparer has approved the document. The approver is notified for final review.
lifecycle.approved: The document has been fully approved and is automatically published.
lifecycle.published: The document is distributed to all users in the distribution list. Read tasks are created for managers.
lifecycle.preparerRejected: The preparer has rejected the document. The revision is locked and a new revision must be created.
lifecycle.approverRejected: The approver has rejected the document. The revision is locked and a new revision must be created.
lifecycle.cancelled: The document is cancelled. All related parties are notified.

revision.title: Revision System
revision.subtitle: How document revisions work:
revision.draftOverwriteDesc: If the current revision is still in Draft status, saving changes will overwrite it instead of creating a new revision.
revision.newRevisionDesc: For published or approved revisions, a new revision is created with an incremented revision number. A new file upload is required.
revision.rejectedRevisionDesc: Rejected revisions are permanently locked. You must create a new revision with updated content to continue the workflow.
revision.revisionNumberDesc: The first revision number can be set manually. All subsequent revisions are automatically numbered (+1).

afterPublish.title: What Happens After Publishing?
afterPublish.notifications: All users in the distribution list receive an email notification about the new document.
afterPublish.readTasks: Managers in the distribution list get a read task. They must open the document and confirm they have read it.
afterPublish.tracking: The document owner can track read status in real time - who has read and who hasn't.
afterPublish.reminders: Automatic reminder emails are sent to managers who haven't confirmed reading within the configured period.
afterPublish.escalation: If a manager still hasn't confirmed after the reminder period, the task is escalated to upper management.

tips.title: Tips
tips.tip1: Use meaningful document codes that follow your organization's naming convention.
tips.tip2: Add a description to help users understand the document's purpose before opening it.
tips.tip3: Select both departments and individual users in the distribution list when needed - duplicates are handled automatically.
tips.tip4: Documents are automatically published after final approval, so review everything carefully before the approver approves.
tips.tip5: Only managers need to confirm reading. Regular users can view published documents without confirmation.
tips.tip6: When the preparer and approver are the same person, the approval process is streamlined to a single step.

carGuide.title: Corrective Action Request Guide
carGuide.subtitle: Learn how to create, manage and close corrective action requests step by step.

creation.title: Creating a CAR
creation.step1Desc: A unique CAR code is auto-generated (e.g., DF-2026-001). You can edit it if needed, but it must remain unique.
creation.step2Desc: Select the source that triggered this corrective action (e.g., Internal Audit, Customer Complaint, Management Review).
creation.step3Desc: Select the management system and the related process where the nonconformity was identified.
creation.step4Desc: Select the department that is raising this corrective action request.
creation.step5Desc: Select the department that will be responsible for resolving the nonconformity.
creation.step6Desc: Assign the person who will be responsible for driving the corrective action to completion.
creation.step7Desc: Describe the nonconformity in detail. Include what was observed, where, and the impact.
creation.step8Desc: Set the target completion date for the corrective action. This will be used for tracking and reminders.
creation.step9Desc: Select users who should receive notifications about status changes throughout the CAR lifecycle.
creation.step10Desc: Review all information and submit to create the CAR. The workflow will start at the Open stage.

workflow.title: Workflow Stages
workflow.subtitle: CARs progress through a 7-stage workflow from creation to closure.
workflow.rejectionNote: If closure is rejected, the CAR returns to the Action Results stage for rework and re-evaluation.
workflow.cancellationNote: Any CAR that has not reached a terminal status (Closed) can be cancelled at any stage.

lifecycle.title: Status Lifecycle
lifecycle.subtitle: Each status represents a specific phase of the corrective action process:
lifecycle.OPEN: Initial state after creation. The CAR has been registered and the responsible person has been notified.
lifecycle.ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYSIS: The team investigates and documents the root cause of the nonconformity using appropriate analysis methods.
lifecycle.IMMEDIATE_ACTION: Immediate corrective actions are taken to contain the issue and prevent further impact.
lifecycle.PLANNED_ACTION: Long-term corrective actions are planned, assigned to team members, and scheduled for implementation.
lifecycle.ACTION_RESULTS: Results of all corrective actions are documented and evaluated for effectiveness.
lifecycle.PENDING_CLOSURE: The CAR is awaiting QA review and closure approval. The reviewer evaluates if all actions were effective.
lifecycle.CLOSED: The CAR has been successfully closed. All corrective actions have been verified as effective.
lifecycle.CANCELLED: The CAR has been cancelled. All related parties are notified.

correctiveActions.title: Corrective Actions Management
correctiveActions.subtitle: How to create and manage corrective actions within a CAR:
correctiveActions.creatingDesc: Create corrective actions with a clear description, assign a responsible person, and set a target completion date.
correctiveActions.teamAssignmentDesc: Add team members to collaborate on corrective actions. Each member can update progress on their assigned tasks.
correctiveActions.statusTrackingDesc: Track each action through its lifecycle: Open, In Progress, and Completed. Monitor overall progress at a glance.
correctiveActions.costTrackingDesc: Record costs associated with each corrective action to track the total cost of quality improvements.

closure.title: Closure Process
closure.subtitle: How the CAR closure and review process works:
closure.whoCanCloseDesc: Only users with the CAR closure permission or administrators can review and close a CAR.
closure.closureCommentDesc: A closure comment and effectiveness evaluation are required. The reviewer must confirm that all actions were effective.
closure.rejectionDesc: If the reviewer determines that actions were insufficient, the CAR is sent back to the Action Results stage for rework.
closure.afterClosureDesc: Once closed, all related parties are notified. The CAR becomes read-only and serves as a permanent record.

roles.title: Roles & Notifications
roles.requesterDesc: Creates the CAR and receives status update notifications throughout the entire lifecycle.
roles.assigneeDesc: The person responsible for resolving the nonconformity. Drives the workflow forward through each stage.
roles.qaReviewerDesc: Reviews the CAR at the Pending Closure stage. Requires CAR closure permission to approve or reject closure.
roles.notificationsDesc: Email and in-app notifications are sent at every status change to keep all stakeholders informed.
roles.remindersDesc: Automatic reminders are sent for overdue CARs to ensure timely completion of corrective actions.

tips.title: Tips
tips.tip1: Provide a detailed nonconformity description to help the responsible team understand the issue quickly.
tips.tip2: Select the correct notification source - this helps with reporting and trend analysis.
tips.tip3: Set realistic target dates that allow enough time for thorough root cause analysis and effective corrective actions.
tips.tip4: Use the root cause analysis stage thoroughly - a well-identified root cause leads to more effective corrective actions.
tips.tip5: Document immediate actions separately from planned actions to distinguish containment from permanent fixes.
tips.tip6: Track costs for each corrective action to build a clear picture of quality improvement investments.
tips.tip7: Add all relevant stakeholders to the notification list so everyone stays informed of progress.
tips.tip8: Review action results carefully before requesting closure - the QA reviewer will verify effectiveness.

car.listDescription: Manage and track corrective action requests

stageGuide.title: What to Do at This Stage
stageGuide.OPEN: The CAR has been created and an assignee has been assigned. Review the nonconformity description and click "Advance" to start the root cause analysis process.
stageGuide.ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYSIS: Investigate and identify the root cause(s) of the nonconformity. Use the Root Cause Analysis section below to document your findings. You can use methods such as 5 Why, Fishbone Diagram, etc.
stageGuide.IMMEDIATE_ACTION: Define and implement immediate containment actions to prevent the nonconformity from spreading. Record all actions taken in the Immediate Action section below.
stageGuide.PLANNED_ACTION: Plan corrective actions to eliminate the root cause. Add corrective action items, assign responsible persons, set target dates and estimated costs. Each action can have a team assigned.
stageGuide.ACTION_RESULTS: Record the results of the planned corrective actions. Complete each action by clicking the action menu (three dots) and selecting "Complete". Enter results and actual costs for each action.
stageGuide.PENDING_CLOSURE: The CAR is awaiting closure review. The authorized reviewer will evaluate the effectiveness of all actions and either approve closure or reject for rework.
stageGuide.CLOSED: The CAR has been successfully closed. All corrective actions have been completed and verified. This record is now read-only.
stageGuide.CANCELLED: This CAR has been cancelled and is now read-only.

car.noRootCause: No root cause analysis added yet
car.deleteActionConfirm: This corrective action will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
car.pendingClosureDescription: This CAR is awaiting closure approval. Please review and either close or reject.
